item.content:encoded: Haha, I love the Boston tea party bit. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, through the doors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a way to get out of this scp?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a couple of grammar and spelling mistakes still present but overall this article is pretty good

I especially like the final log and dr. papertainment's statement that we are still fighting the good fight

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed an SCP-4000 into an SCP-4890. Other than that, great article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like the cowboy vs a t-rex .. jsut read that scp so it was a good nod

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the idea is rather intriguing and funny, I find myself underwhelmed by the execution. The article left much to be desired, as it was far too brief and it provided pretty much no new information or insight. There was no story or world building here, just an opportunity for OP to make some quick historical jokes and have some fun writing a fairly generic Wondertainment character.

+ Long winded critique
- Close my ramblings

I think the first thing that took me out of it was the description. There are lots of awkward sentences and improper word usage that just kind of kills the scientific efficacy of the article.

The back of the cover there is a small pocket holding SCP-4890-1. SCP-4890-1 is a 0.147 meters tall origami depicting an individual with a purple suit, bow tie, and hat along with a cane. On the torso piece, is a small stylized W.

SCP-4890's anomalous properties occur when it is opened within a room. The room in which it is opened will begin to convert to an infinite purple space. An invisible barrier matching the measurements of the room will prevent any exploration and a door will be present to allow exit or entry into the altered environment. As this occurs, SCP-4890-1 will activate by being shrouded in a large puff of smoke instantaneously to become 1.4 meters tall and animate.

Nearly every sentence that I've quoted above has some kind of grammatical error. Consider editing it to something along the lines of:

On the back cover of the book, there is a pocket containing SCP-4890-1. SCP-4890-1 is an origami man wearing a purple suit, bow tie, and hat, measuring 0.147m in height. SCP-4890-1 has a small, stylized "W" on the front pocket of his suit jacket and is carrying a purple cane.

SCP-4890's anomalous properties occur when the book is opened by a sapient being in a confined space. The room that SCP-4890 was opened in will morph to a seemingly infinite purple space, hereafter referred to as SCP-4890-2. The subject who opened the book will be limited in movement to the dimensions of the space they were in prior to the opening of SCP-4890. A door will be present in SCP-4890-2 to allow exit from the spatial anomaly. When a subject is transported to SCP-4890-2, SCP-4890-1 will disappear into a puff of purple smoke, from which it emerges 1.47m in height and fully animate.

As you can see, I took the liberty of making the vast purple space SCP-4890-2, as the spatial anomaly needs to be referred to a bunch of times, and you gave us nothing to refer to it by. Giving it a designation just makes writing easier and it sounds better.

This is just an example of what could be done to make the description read easier. I also did not put the entire description in here for obvious reasons, but the whole article could use some revisions. Commas are missing here and there, some words are in the wrong places, and some sentences just need some reworking in general.

In addition, you might want to think about adding more to the story. There isn't a recovery log (which usually are an interesting part of Wondertainment SCPs), the "twist" is pretty bland, and there is no backstory regarding why Dr. Wondertainment would even bother making such a book. Adding more relevancy to this SCP could help it stand on its own as a unique piece made by Dr. Wondertainment rather than a forgotten relic of obscurity.

Perhaps if this article gets some major edits/additions, I will revisit it, but for now, it's a -1 for me.

EDITS: misspellings and minor grammar mistakes as I caught them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a couple really funny jokes here (the Velociraptor stood out), but I feel like there could be more done with this. Many of the entries are just kind of "there" (the Hindenburg one is an example).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second paragraph of the description made me a bit confused but the logs cleared that up. Logs usually just add without explaining more. +1

item.title: Hmmm
item.content:encoded:
Humans (and their "siblings") won the war once. Does that mean they can win it again? Find out on the next episode of Dragon Ball Z.

I am giving it a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. Also, yeah, the GOI always made me smile too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was an interesting idea, but I think it needs more stuff in the file for imrpoved execution. However, I reccomend you do a grammar check on it. I found a few incorrections, mostly in the interview. Not typos, just places where you could have added a transition word or comma. Speaking of the interview, it could have been a lot better! The Foundation fellow doesn't really ask questions, he just goes to some time periods. Unlike other SCP articles, the interviewer is not inquisitive towards the anomaly. For example, I think he should ask Dr. Papertainment how he does the stuff with the room, if he is aware that he defies scientific laws, or why he won't delve deeper into the history he presents? Also, does the book make people hallucinate the purple room, or does it actually happen? So much to add here! Sorry that I went on a tangent.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s so sad that not a lot of Wondertainment is being made for the site anymore, as the GoI brings a smile to my face. Great work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. Paint me underwhelmed.

The anomaly here is pretty stock Wondertainment shenanigans, which is a little bland as the article doesn't push the GoI in any particularly interesting directions, but rather seems to tread over the same whimsical ground often covered by these articles.

While the Hindenberg Disaster and Assassination of Ceaser were fairly clever test log entries, the rest fell flat and seemed more like an attempt to link out to other historical objects without much of an attempt to build a substantial narrative connecting the pieces.

Finally, the twist at the end featuring SCP-1000 has just been done so much before as to take all weight out of that particular twist.

-1. Perhaps if there had been more logs of the quick, witty quality as the ones mentioned above I'd be a little more willing to let other aspects of the article slide, but as is you don't do much here for me as a reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some suggestions for events:

  • Hitler's Suicide
  • The Creation of the Universe
  • Something relating to a real life religion
  • The Invention of the Wheel (I would make this comedic)
  • The creation of various SCPs, such as SCP-682, SCP-173, or even something like SCP-2317

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damm, for a cold post this is pretty good. Nice work.

item.title: Re: Fancy dinosaur link is broken.
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I now fixed it.

item.title: Fancy dinosaur link is broken.
item.content:encoded:
Should be "http://www.scp-wiki.net/a-fancy-dinosaur" without the "-lucky" at the end.

Gonna upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it should. Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I won't upvote right now, since I'm more critical during contests (I was forced to downvote the funniest thing in this site just because Bush vs. Gore belongs with the -Js… it would be one of the best tough) but this is not too silly for the mainlist, altough it is too short to be 4000.
No vote, but I'll upvote after the end of the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice article, but one quick spelling error.

[A specimen of Triceratops prorsus is seen existing from behind bushes and moves out of view behind more bushes.]

Should Existing be Exiting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am going to later add some additional events. So far, I have the following in mind.

  • The Birth of Nikola Tesla
  • The Creation of SCP-4000-1 itself
  • One of the concerts of The Beatles or just something relating to them.
  • Site 13
  • The sinking of the Titanic
  • The Marianas Trench
  • Hitler while writing Mein Kampf
  • The creation of chocolate
  • The destruction of the Library of Alexandria

Suggestions would be appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't much like this at first, but dammit, I can't hate Dr. Papertainment.

SCP-4000-1 claimed and visually displayed that Julius Caesar was not assassinated and established several businesses. Businesses include Caesar's Palace in Las Vegas, and the pizza chain Little Caesars.

And this is one of the funniest things I have ever read on this site.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only problem is that that this is not long enough. Which is probably a good sign, but man I would love to see 4000-1 talk about so many different events.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is such a cute idea, I love it dearly.
+1 without a doubt.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
It's a coldpost which I know may not last long but the dead line is just so close.

Nonetheless, here is my entry. I may add more stuff to the test log if I can come up with stuff.

Edit: Huh, I guess it may be staying around. Looks like a good start.
